text:
It's obvious that most of you have never seen this movie. The trailer is deliberately misleading and includes footage that isn't in the actual movie. She never gets pregnant, and she isn't the victim of gang rape. Her boyfriend has a crush on the health teacher (4:12) so Arlene Sue tells him she's pregnant in order to win him back. 

The "gang rape" scene is just a jealous ex-boyfriend that wrestles her to the ground while others stand around and watch. Although it does look like he's trying to take her shirt off, he never actually does. Earlier in the movie the same ex-boyfriend tries to rape the health teacher, but he's thwarted and escapes. The narrator is trying to make this movie sound more brutal, salacious and explicit than it actually is in order to fill seats. Exploitation cinema in a nutshell.  

There IS, however, a graphic scene featuring an actual live birth, with the vagina fully visible. It's not Arlene's. The health teacher is basically accused of peddling pornography, so she's brought before a disciplinary committee where the footage is shown to clear her name (the footage has educational value, afterall). In some respects, this trailer is more scandalous than the actual movie.
